serviceDescription: Amazon Aurora is a MySQL and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built for the cloud, that combines the performance and availability of traditional enterprise databases with the simplicity and cost-effectiveness of open source databases.

templates.title: Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible Edition
templates.description: Provides speed and availability of high-end commercial databases with the simplicity and cost-effectiveness of open source databases.
templates.helpText: Amazon Aurora is a MySQL and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built for the cloud, that combines the performance and availability of traditional enterprise databases with the simplicity and cost-effectiveness of open source databases.

cards.title: Cluster Configuration Option
cards.description: Database instance, storage and IO charges will vary based on the configuration. Review Amazon Aurora pricing for more information

components.content:
Cost effective pricing for many applications with moderate I/O usage (I/O costs <25% of total database costs).
Pay-per-request I/O charges apply. DB instance and storage prices don't include I/O usage.

Predictable pricing for all applications. Improved price-performance for I/O-intensive applications (I/O costs >25% of total database costs).
No additional charges for read/write I/O operations. DB instance and storage prices includes I/O usage.
Aurora automatically accounts for the price difference between the Aurora Standard and Aurora I/O-Optimized Reserved Instances. To learn more, visit Aurora reserved DB instance billing examples in the Aurora User Guide.

cards.title: Aurora PostgreSQL Compatible DB instance specifications
cards.helpText: Amazon Aurora is up to five times faster than standard MySQL databases and three times faster than standard PostgreSQL databases. It provides the security, availability, and reliability of commercial databases at 1/10th the cost.

row.label: Quantity
row.description: Enter the number of DB instances that you need including read replicas.

row.label: Utilization
row.description: With utilization, you still have to stop the instance to get the cost benefit. Utilization only affects OnDemand pricing for instances and not the storage, backups, etc.

cards.title: Database Storage
cards.helpText: Storage consumed by your Amazon Aurora database is billed in per GB-month increments and IOs consumed are billed in per million request increments.

components.description: Total Storage Size of the Aurora Cluster

components.label: Baseline IO rate
components.description: Enter the number of IOs per second that your workload consumes during off-peak periods.

components.label: Peak IO rate
components.description: Enter the maximum number of IOs per second your workload consumes during peak periods.

components.label: Usage alert
components.content: Peak IO rate must be greater than or equal to Baseline IO rate

components.label: Duration of peak IO activity
components.description: Enter the number of hours per month when your cluster or instance workload operates at peak.

cards.title: Backup Storage
cards.helpText: Backup storage for Amazon Aurora is the storage associated with your automated database backups and any customer-initiated DB cluster snapshots.There is no additional charge for backup storage of up to 100% of your total Aurora database storage for each Aurora DB cluster. There is also no additional charge for backup storage if your backup retention period is 1 day and you don’t have any snapshots beyond the retention period.

cards.title: Snapshot Export
cards.helpText: Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) Snapshot Export provides an automated method to export data within an Amazon RDS or Aurora snapshot to Amazon S3 in Parquet format.

components.content: Amazon S3 charges for the exported file are extra and not included in this calculation

components.label: Total Size of Backup Processed for Export (GB)

templates.title: Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL Limitless Database
templates.description: Scales write throughput and storage by distributing a database workload over multiple Aurora writer instances, while maintaining the ability to use it as a single database.
